title: Question library + project-type taxonomy + PROJECT.md schema/writer
description:
Second ticket in the project-intake wizard chain (TASK-010..015). Builds on `src/question-engine.js` (TASK-010) to deliver the actual question catalog the wizard will ask, the curated project-type taxonomy locked in the 2026-05-26 design conversation, and the PROJECT.md persistence layer that captures the answers.

Deliverables:

1. **`src/question-library.js`** тАФ exports `COMMON_QUESTIONS` (тЙе6 questions covering project name, description, type, target_users, success_criteria, primary_use_cases) and `TYPE_SPECIFIC_QUESTIONS` (an object keyed by project_type values, each value an array of questions gated by `when: a => a.project_type === '<type>'`). Also exports a helper that returns the full combined array suitable for one `runQuestionnaire` invocation.

2. **Project-type taxonomy** locked as the enum `['web-saas', 'cli-tool', 'data-pipeline', 'ml-model', 'library', 'other']`. Every type except 'other' has тЙе3 type-specific questions; 'other' has 1 free-text question capturing the user's own description (LLM inference happens downstream in TASK-013).

3. **`src/project-md.js`** тАФ exports `writeProjectMd({repoRoot, answers, now})` and `readProjectMd({repoRoot})`. PROJECT.md lives at `<repoRoot>/PROJECT.md`. Format: minimal YAML frontmatter (name, type, created_at, schema_version) + markdown body with prose sections per answer. Round-trip lossless: write then read returns the same answers map.

4. **`state/PROJECT.schema.json`** тАФ JSON Schema for the frontmatter shape. `schema_version: 1` to enable future migration.

5. **No external YAML dep.** Constrain the frontmatter to a subset (string-or-array-of-strings scalars) so we can write a 20-line in-house parser. Avoids dragging in js-yaml or gray-matter.

All writes go through `atomicWriteFile` from src/atomic-write.js. Question definitions reuse the engine's declarative shape; `when` predicates handle the project_type branching.
acceptance_criteria: `src/question-library.js` exports `COMMON_QUESTIONS` (тЙе6 entries) and `TYPE_SPECIFIC_QUESTIONS` (object keyed by project_type values). A helper `buildIntakeQuestions()` returns the concatenated array ready for `runQuestionnaire`.
The `project_type` enum is exactly `['web-saas', 'cli-tool', 'data-pipeline', 'ml-model', 'library', 'other']` тАФ verified by a test that asserts the literal array contents.

Every project_type except 'other' has тЙе3 type-specific questions, each gated by `when: a => a.project_type === '<type>'`; the 'other' branch has exactly 1 free-text question for architecture description.

`src/project-md.js` exports `writeProjectMd({repoRoot, answers, now})` and `readProjectMd({repoRoot})`. Round-trip test: `writeProjectMd(answers)` then `readProjectMd()` returns an equivalent answers map (same keys, same values).
`writeProjectMd` uses `atomicWriteFile` from src/atomic-write.js тАФ verified via `vi.mock('node:fs')` and the same fsync-before-rename invariant used in TASK-001 and TASK-010.
PROJECT.md format is documented in src/project-md.js with a working example and parses with the in-house minimal parser (no external dep added to package.json).
`state/PROJECT.schema.json` exists, declares `schema_version: 1` as required, and validates a freshly written PROJECT.md frontmatter via ajv (same pattern as `tests/live-state.spec.js`).

End-to-end test: run `runQuestionnaire(buildIntakeQuestions())` with a fake prompter scripted for the 'web-saas' branch, then `writeProjectMd(answers)`, then `readProjectMd`, then ajv-validate frontmatter. All steps succeed in one test.

comments.author: orchestrator
comments.body: Created. Design fixed pre-tests: taxonomy enum locked as [web-saas, cli-tool, data-pipeline, ml-model, library, other]; questions are declarative (engine-compatible shape from TASK-010); type-specific branching uses `when: a => a.project_type === 'X'` so the whole intake runs in one engine invocation; PROJECT.md uses minimal YAML frontmatter (no external dep, in-house ~20-line parser constrained to string-or-array-of-strings scalars) + markdown body for prose. Workflow: developer writes failing Vitest suite covering all 8 ACs, then implements src/question-library.js + src/project-md.js + state/PROJECT.schema.json, then reviewer in fresh context. No bin/init.js wiring in this ticket тАФ that's TASK-012.
